instruction: Score this FLS Task 5 intracorporeal suture attempt. Analyze the frames and produce a structured scoring result.
input:
Frame 1 (needle_load): At 7 seconds, both instruments in field near drain. Pink/purple curved suture clearly visible draped across drain and block. Needle loading in progress — fast entry.
Frame 4 (suture_placement): At 28 seconds, both instruments at the drain. Suture has been placed through both sides of the drain — purple suture threads visible exiting both sides. Completing placement.
Frame 6 (first_throw): At 42 seconds, first throw in progress. Both suture ends (pink/purple) clearly visible extending from the drain. Instruments wrapping suture for surgeon's knot. The drain slit is still visible and open.
Frame 8 (first_throw): At 56 seconds, first throw COMPLETED and tightened. Instruments spread wide apart (left upper-left, right upper-right) in classic post-tightening posture. Knot X pattern clearly visible on drain. Drain slit beginning to close.
Frame 10 (second_throw): At 70 seconds, second throw tightening. Both instruments pulling suture tails laterally in mirror posture. Knot visible on drain. Drain slit closing around the knot.
Frame 13 (third_throw): At 91 seconds, third throw in progress or completing. Right instrument across drain, left instrument at upper left. Knot clearly visible with blue/purple suture forming cross pattern. Drain slit appears mostly closed.
Frame 17 (suture_cut): At 118 seconds, cutting phase. Both instruments holding suture tails laterally — left pulling left, right pulling right. Suture taut between instruments. Knot visible with dark cross pattern. Drain slit appears CLOSED with good approximation.
Frame 23 (completion): Final frame at 139 seconds. Instruments fully withdrawn. EXCELLENT final result: compact knot clearly visible with purple suture cross pattern. Short, well-trimmed suture tails. Drain slit appears FULLY CLOSED — edges well approximated around the knot. NO VISIBLE GAP. Drain not avulsed, remains centered on suture block. Best visible final result in the monitor-recorded series.

Bilateral spread-tighten posture confirms good technique. Confidence moderate."}, {"throw_number": 2, "is_surgeon_knot": null, "is_single_throw": true, "hand_used": "bilateral", "hand_switched": true, "appears_secure": true, "notes": "Mirror tightening posture at 70s. Drain edges approximating. Confidence moderate."}, {"throw_number": 3, "is_surgeon_knot": null, "is_single_throw": true, "hand_used": "unclear", "hand_switched": true, "appears_secure": true, "notes": "Third throw completed by ~95s. Drain closure appears complete after this throw. Confidence moderate."}], "suture_placement": {"deviation_from_mark1_mm": 1.5, "deviation_from_mark2_mm": 1.5, "total_deviation_penalty": 3.0, "confidence": "low"}, "drain_assessment": {"gap_visible": false, "drain_avulsed": false, "slit_closure_quality": "closed", "assessment_note": "Best drain closure visible in the monitor-recorded series. The final frame shows edges well approximated around a compact knot with no visible gap. Purple suture makes assessment easier than black suture in V1-V10."}, "estimated_penalties": 3.0, "estimated_fls_score": 462.0, "technique_summary": "V13 confirms the trainee has leveled up. At 135 seconds with NO visible gap and a compact, well-trimmed knot, this represents the best combined speed-and-quality performance in the series. While V12 was 20 seconds faster (115s), V13's superior visible quality (fully closed slit, short tails) may mean a higher true FLS score once penalties are accurately measured. The suture placement at 22 seconds is a new fastest, and all three throw phases are efficient and well-tightened. Cutting at 40 seconds remains the longest phase and the primary target for further improvement. The purple suture significantly improves scoring visibility compared to the black suture used in V1-V10."}

instruction: Score this FLS Task 5 intracorporeal suture attempt. Analyze the frames and produce a structured scoring result.
input:
Frame 1 (needle_load): Both instruments already in field at drain. Left instrument from lower left, right instrument from right (trainee's gloved hand visible holding it). Curved needle with black suture at drain. White suture loop visible at drain. Heavy suture debris on perforated board above foam — many prior attempts evident.
Frame 2 (suture_placement): Needle driving through drain. Both instruments at drain. Curved needle visible passing through tissue. Drain is being lifted significantly during the pass — good counter-traction by left instrument. White suture (the drain) being manipulated.
Frame 3 (suture_placement): Continuing needle pass. Both instruments engaged at drain level. Needle being driven through second mark. Drain lifted substantially — vigorous but controlled manipulation.
Frame 4 (suture_placement): Suture PLACED. Both dark suture ends clearly visible extending from drain — one going left (with a loop resting on the gauze), one going right. Instruments retracted from field. Clean view of placed suture through drain.
Frame 5 (first_throw): Instruments re-entering field for first throw. Left instrument from left, right from right. Both dark suture ends visible extending from drain, widely splayed. Starting to position for first throw wrap.
Frame 6 (first_throw): First throw wrapping in progress. Both instruments near drain. Suture loops visible — white loop at right side (suture being wrapped). Dark suture visible crossing at drain. Instruments working above drain level.
Frame 7 (second_throw): First throw completed and tightened. Both instruments at drain level. Knot visible with dark suture X/cross pattern at drain center. Suture ends extending from knot. Instruments repositioning for second throw.
Frame 8 (second_throw): Second throw tightening. Left instrument from upper left pulling. Knot visible at center. Suture tails extending to lower right. Second throw being cinched down.
Frame 9 (third_throw): Third throw in progress. Both instruments at drain. Suture wrapping visible — loops of dark suture around drain area. Left instrument from upper left. Purple/colored highlight visible at instrument tip. Large suture loops.
Frame 10 (third_throw): Third throw completing. Knot visible at drain center with dark suture crossing pattern. Tails extending left and right. Both instruments at sides. Drain appears well-approximated with knot seated and slit closing.
Frame 11 (suture_cut): Early cutting phase. EXCELLENT VIEW of knot quality — dark suture X/cross pattern clearly visible at drain center. Drain slit appears WELL CLOSED around the knot with good approximation. The drain is slightly pinched (hourglass shape) showing good knot tension. Long suture tails extending — one to lower left, one curving down. Scissors at lower left beginning first cut.
Frame 12 (suture_cut): Mid-cutting. Instrument from upper left at drain. Knot visible. Tails shorter than at 108s — one short tail at upper left, one longer to right. Progress on cutting.
Frame 13 (suture_cut): Final cuts completing. Both instruments at drain. Knot visible with SHORT stubs. Suture tails appear well-trimmed — much shorter than V20/V21 final frames. Drain slit well-closed.
Frame 14 (completion): TASK COMPLETE. Instruments withdrawn. Clean final view: Compact knot at drain center with dark suture X pattern. SHORT well-trimmed suture tails (stubs only). Drain slit WELL CLOSED — excellent approximation around the knot. Drain slightly compressed (hourglass shape) confirming secure knot tension. Drain centered on block, NOT avulsed. No visible gap. Red-handled instrument (scissors?) visible resting on desk below monitor — confirms instruments withdrawn.
output: {"completion_time_seconds": 152.0, "phase_timings": [{"phase": "idle", "start_seconds": 0.0, "end_seconds": 3.0, "duration_seconds": 3.0}, {"phase": "needle_load", "start_seconds": 3.0, "end_seconds": 8.0, "duration_seconds": 5.0}, {"phase": "suture_placement", "start_seconds": 8.0, "end_seconds": 28.0, "duration_seconds": 20.0}, {"phase": "first_throw", "start_seconds": 28.0, "end_seconds": 58.0, "duration_seconds": 30.0}, {"phase": "second_throw", "start_seconds": 58.0, "end_seconds": 75.0, "duration_seconds": 17.0}, {"phase": "third_throw", "start_seconds": 75.0, "end_seconds": 98.0, "duration_seconds": 23.0}, {"phase": "suture_cut", "start_seconds": 98.0, "end_seconds": 155.0, "duration_seconds": 57.0}], "knot_assessments": [{"throw_number": 1, "is_surgeon_knot": true, "is_single_throw": null, "hand_used": "unclear", "hand_switched": null, "appears_secure": true, "notes": "First throw 28-58s (30s). Longest throw \u2014 includes some repositioning time. Double wrap visible consistent with surgeon's knot. Knot seated well. Black suture easier to track. Confidence: moderate."}, {"throw_number": 2, "is_surgeon_knot": null, "is_single_throw": true, "hand_used": "unclear", "hand_switched": true, "appears_secure": true, "notes": "Second throw 58-75s (17s). Efficient. Single throw. Hand switch appears present. Confidence: low-moderate."}, {"throw_number": 3, "is_surgeon_knot": null, "is_single_throw": true, "hand_used": "unclear", "hand_switched": true, "appears_secure": true, "notes": "Third throw 75-98s (23s). Moderate pace. Hand switch appears present. Knot well-secured after this throw \u2014 drain compression visible. Confidence: low-moderate."}], "suture_placement": {"deviation_from_mark1_mm": 1.5, "deviation_from_mark2_mm": 1.5, "total_deviation_penalty": 3.0, "confidence": "low"}, "drain_assessment": {"gap_visible": false, "drain_avulsed": false, "slit_closure_quality": "well_closed", "assessment_note": "BEST drain closure assessment in recent series. At 108s, drain slit clearly well-closed with hourglass compression from knot tension. At 161s final frame, slit remains well-closed with compact centered knot. No visible gap. Short trimmed tails. Drain not avulsed, centered on block. Confidence: moderate \u2014 black suture and Insignia monitor provide better visibility than recent RCA recordings."}, "estimated_penalties": 5.0, "estimated_fls_score": 443.0, "technique_summary": "V22 at 152 seconds is a SIGNIFICANT IMPROVEMENT and the best performance since V15 (142s). This is recorded at a NEW LOCATION (Insignia monitor, different from RCA setup), with BLACK suture providing much better visual tracking. Heavy suture debris indicates multiple prior attempts in this session, yet the trainee still performed well \u2014 suggesting either good fatigue management or the benefits of warming up. Suture placement at 20 seconds is EXCELLENT. Throws at 70 seconds total are mid-range but effective. Cutting at 57 seconds remains the dominant bottleneck (37% of total time) but CRITICALLY, the QUALITY of cutting is much improved \u2014 the final frame shows SHORT, well-trimmed tails, the best cutting outcome in 5+ videos. The overall result quality (compact knot, well-closed drain, short tails, no gap, no avulsion) is the best since V14/V15. This video suggests the trainee benefits from practicing at this alternate location and/or with black suture."}

instruction: Score this FLS Task 5 intracorporeal suture attempt. Analyze the frames and produce a structured scoring result.
input:
Frame 46 (needle_load): First instrument becomes visible and the task begins.
Frame 241 (needle_load): The needle is loaded and being aligned toward the Penrose targets for the initial bite.
Frame 616 (suture_placement): The needle has traversed both sides of the slit and the suture is being pulled through the marked area.
Frame 1411 (first_throw): The first throw is being formed and cinched down at the Penrose.
Frame 2251 (second_throw): The second throw is being laid down to reverse the first throw and square the knot.
Frame 3271 (third_throw): The final throw is being formed and tightened to secure the knot.
Frame 3901 (suture_cut): The knot is seated and the suture tails are being presented for intracorporeal cutting.
Frame 4234 (completion): Both tails have just been divided and the instruments begin to withdraw.

two wraps appear visible before cinching, consistent with a surgeon's knot."}, {"throw_number": 2, "is_surgeon_knot": null, "is_single_throw": true, "hand_used": "right", "hand_switched": true, "appears_secure": true, "notes": "Single throw appears to reverse the first throw and lie down appropriately; right-hand lead is the best estimate, with only moderate confidence."}, {"throw_number": 3, "is_surgeon_knot": null, "is_single_throw": true, "hand_used": "left", "hand_switched": true, "appears_secure": true, "notes": "Final single throw appears to alternate back and square the knot; left-hand lead is the best estimate."}], "suture_placement": {"deviation_from_mark1_mm": 1.0, "deviation_from_mark2_mm": 1.5, "total_deviation_penalty": 2.5, "confidence": "low"}, "drain_assessment": {"gap_visible": false, "drain_avulsed": false, "slit_closure_quality": "complete", "assessment_note": "No visible gap at the knot site, drain not avulsed, knot stack remains seated through cut."}, "estimated_penalties": 2.5, "estimated_fls_score": 457.9, "technique_summary": "The stitch placement appears close to the marked targets and the drain remains securely attached throughout the task. The final knot appears seated without an obvious slit gap or gross slip, but the run is slowed by repeated regrasping/reset motions during knot tying and a relatively long tail-management phase. Hand alternation appears to have been attempted on throws 2 and 3, although that is the lowest-confidence element of this assessment from the top-down view. This is a strong pass-level second attempt on the blue trainer."}

instruction: Score this FLS Task 5 intracorporeal suture attempt. Analyze the frames and produce a structured scoring result.
input:

Frame 7 (needle_load): Hands visible loading needle with suture outside the trainer box
Frame 10 (suture_placement): First instrument entering trainer field - timer starts, can see Penrose drain with marks
Frame 12 (suture_placement): Needle being passed through first mark on Penrose drain
Frame 13 (suture_placement): Suture being pulled through first puncture site
Frame 14 (first_throw): Beginning surgeon's knot formation - creating double wrap for first throw
Frame 16 (second_throw): Completing second throw with hand switching
Frame 18 (third_throw): Final throw being completed with proper hand switching
Frame 20 (suture_cut): Using scissors to cut suture ends close to knot
Frame 22 (completion): Task completed with knot secured and drain slit closed

Frame 1 (needle_load): Initial setup with needle driver and suture visible. Trainee loading needle onto needle driver.
Frame 3 (suture_placement): Trainee beginning to place suture through first mark on Penrose drain. Needle positioned at drainage mark.
Frame 6 (suture_placement): Continuing suture placement through drain tissue. Both ends of suture visible through the slit drain.
Frame 8 (suture_placement): Completing second suture placement through second mark. Suture now passes through both marked locations.
Frame 10 (first_throw): Beginning first throw of surgeon's knot. Creating initial double throw with right hand manipulation.
Frame 12 (first_throw): Completing surgeon's knot double throw. Knot formation appears secure with good tension.
Frame 14 (second_throw): Starting second throw with hand switch. Switching from right to left hand for opposite direction throw.
Frame 16 (third_throw): Executing third and final throw. Another hand switch performed for alternating throw direction.
Frame 18 (suture_cut): Preparing to cut suture ends. Scissors positioned near completed knot.
Frame 22 (completion): Task completed with both suture ends cut. Final knot visible on drain with no gap in slit.

Frame 8 (first_throw): Continued C-loop manipulation with instruments working to form the initial throw.
Frame 10 (first_throw): First throw appears complete with instruments positioning for tightening.
Frame 12 (first_throw): First throw being tightened with text overlay 'Unlocking the knot'.
Frame 14 (first_throw): Text indicates 'Sliding the knot' - demonstrating the slip knot mechanism.
Frame 15 (first_throw): Red line highlighting the locking mechanism with 'Locking the knot' text.
Frame 17 (second_throw): Beginning second throw with C-loop formation indicated by text overlay.
Frame 19 (second_throw): Second throw construction in progress with good instrument coordination.
Frame 22 (completion): Final view showing completed knot in center of field. No instruments visible.
